1.content:
- [H-01] lib.rs - Transaction IDs stored in temporary storage enables replay
attacks
Solution: None
Reason: The Transaction ID is used to map the on-chain data to the project's Backend (BE) services. Since only the authorized wallet can call the contract to mint or release funds, an attacker cannot exploit the system without possessing that wallet. Furthermore, we use temporary storage instead of persistent storage because we only want to prevent the system wallet from duplicating and sending the same transaction at the same time, and using persistent will be way too expensive. Otherwise, if a user intends to reuse the Transaction ID outside the system after a successful operation, the amount of tokens sent to the contract would be locked without a corresponding mint or release on the destination chain.
Status: Not implement

Solution: None
Reason: Using persistent storage instead of temporary storage would significantly increase the transaction cost for the user. Furthermore, the relayer will not cause a duplicate transaction after a recovery; a new transaction could only be created manually.
Status: Not implement
